evidence.description: Lines 17-25 import subscribeEditingSessionState and createBlockEditorAdapter; line 31 instantiates blockEditorAdapter; line 32 holds editingSessionState in $state; lines 58-65 wire subscribeEditingSessionState in $effect with cleanup; lines 116-123 pass both as props to FeedList. Resolves FIND-S5-PHASE3-001 wiring gap.

Lines 23-24, 42-45 declare editingSessionState/blockEditorAdapter Props; lines 53-54 destructure with default null; lines 403-404 forward both to each FeedRow. Resolves FIND-S5-PHASE3-001 forwarding gap.

Lines 1117-1122 amended REQ-FEED-031 EARS to require `{ noteId, type, content, issuedAt }` (matching adapter type) and clarify that the client UUID rides only on dispatchFocusBlock.blockId. PARTIAL fix — see FIND-S5-PHASE3-iter2-001 for AC-level inconsistency that remains at line 1173.

Lines 842-881 PROP-FEED-S5-019 now performs 3 clicks: (1) idle→click 1 dispatches (line 854), (2) remount in switching state, click 2 NOT dispatched (line 872), (3) click 3 NOT dispatched (line 876). Although the test uses two separate mounts (with separate adapters) to model the editing→switching transition, both halves of the race-suppression invariant are exercised — first click dispatches, subsequent clicks during switching are suppressed. This addresses FIND-S5-PHASE3-003 within the constraints of FeedRow being a presentational component.

Reviewed lines 1-359. PROP-FEED-S5-011 idempotency scenarios (a)-(e) exercise the FeedRow $effect via FeedRowSprint5Wrapper for cases (b)/(d) and direct mount for (a)/(c)/(e). Scenario (d) (FIND-iter2-005) verifies undefined→non-empty→undefined produces a fresh UUID.
Lines 58-65: subscribeEditingSessionState wired in $effect; cleanup returns unsubscribe. No reactive deps inside the effect, so it runs once on mount and cleans up on unmount. Correct.

Lines 250-310 $effect logic for fallback restart conditions: blocksPresent path resets fallbackAppliedFor and sets lastBlocksWasNonEmpty=true (line 269-273); absent path checks (i) null state, (ii) noteId switch, (iii) prior non-empty cycle (line 277-279); guards for null adapter at line 287; both await calls in try/catch at lines 289-308. Logic correct and matches REQ-FEED-031 EARS.
Lines 27-64: 16 dispatch methods with documented Tauri command names; each payload includes issuedAt. Matches REQ-FEED-030 command-mapping table. Now actually instantiated in +page.svelte (line 31).
Lines 34-54: synchronous handler (no await/then/setTimeout/queueMicrotask inside listen callback); INBOUND only (no invoke import). Now actually consumed by +page.svelte (line 59).

Audit script unchanged; PROP-FEED-S5-001/002/003/004/012/014/015/017/021 still enforced. Listener count (PROP-FEED-S5-003) still equals 1 because +page.svelte uses subscribeEditingSessionState() not listen() directly.
Single-column layout (REQ-FEED-028) preserved; .feed-main wrapper, no grid-template-columns, height: 100vh present (line 147).

Spec REMAINS internally inconsistent after iter-2 fix: REQ-FEED-031 EARS (line 1119) now correctly says `{ noteId, type: 'paragraph', content: '', issuedAt }`, but the Acceptance Criteria block at line 1173 STILL reads `dispatchInsertBlockAtBeginning({ noteId, newBlock: { id: <UUID>, block_type: 'paragraph', content: '' }, issuedAt: <ISO> })`. The fix amended the EARS narrative but failed to amend the AC enumeration that PROP-FEED-S5-011 traces to (FIND-S5-PHASE3-iter2-001). FIND-S5-PHASE3-004 is therefore only PARTIALLY resolved.

New 8-test suite asserts the wiring chain via source grep: +page.svelte imports + subscribe call (with $effect cleanup regex) + adapter instantiation + FeedList prop pass; FeedList declares props + forwards to FeedRow; FeedRow declares props + uses {#if shouldMountBlocks && blockEditorAdapter} mount gate. This matches the alternative path explicitly granted in FIND-S5-PHASE3-002.expectedBehavior ('Equivalently, the source-grep gate should assert presence of subscribeEditingSessionState( and createBlockEditorAdapter( in production source').

PROP-FEED-S5-019 rewritten to perform real first click on idle (asserts dispatchSelectPastNote.toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1)) followed by two clicks on switching state (asserts dispatchSelectPastNote.not.toHaveBeenCalled). Adequate verification of REQ-FEED-006 click suppression invariant given FeedRow's stateless nature.

criteriaResults.rationale: +page.svelte single-column layout, height:100vh, no forbidden identifiers; main-route.dom.vitest.ts source-structure assertions pass. No regression from iter-1.

Listener exists exactly once in editingSessionChannel.ts and is async-free. +page.svelte now invokes subscribeEditingSessionState in $effect (lines 58-65) with cleanup. main-route-wiring.dom.vitest.ts asserts the call presence + cleanup regex. Resolves iter-1 FIND-S5-PHASE3-001 and iter-1 FIND-S5-PHASE3-002 for this CRIT.

+page.svelte instantiates blockEditorAdapter via createBlockEditorAdapter() (line 31) and passes it through FeedList (line 122) → FeedRow (FeedList line 404). The mount gate `shouldMountBlocks && blockEditorAdapter` (FeedRow line 448) can now fire in production. main-route-wiring.dom.vitest.ts asserts the entire wiring chain. Resolves iter-1 FIND-S5-PHASE3-001/002 for this CRIT.

Fallback dispatch chain is reachable in production now (CRIT-202 wiring resolved). Implementation matches the EARS shape `{ noteId, type, content, issuedAt }`. However, the spec AC at behavioral-spec.md:1173 still enumerates the OLD shape `{ noteId, newBlock: { id, block_type, content }, issuedAt }`. EARS (line 1119) and AC (line 1173) disagree within the SAME requirement (FIND-S5-PHASE3-iter2-001). The iter-1 FIND-S5-PHASE3-004 is only partially resolved — the EARS was fixed, the AC enumeration was not.
Sprint-4 emit-order baseline preserved; editingSessionChannel handler is synchronous. REQ-FEED-032 invariant intact. No regression from iter-1.

Forbidden-identifier audit (PROP-FEED-S5-014) and src/lib/editor/ filesystem absence (PROP-FEED-S5-015) both clean. The new wiring imports introduced no forbidden identifiers (subscribeEditingSessionState and createBlockEditorAdapter are not on the forbidden list).

Sub-assertions a/b/c/d operate against direct-mounted FeedRow with hand-injected blockEditorAdapter; they remain valid Sprint 5 known-constraint coverage. Production blockEditorAdapter is now wired so the same surface is reachable in production.

PROP-FEED-S5-019 rewritten to perform real first click on idle followed by suppressed clicks on switching state. PROP-FEED-S5-018 row-level remount continuity still verified. PROP-FEED-S5-020 imports the channel module directly (acceptable for EC-FEED-020 timing-edge proof).
